heading: Salvage claims by United States
label: 7804 Salvage claims by United States
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)480410:1863., .Oct. 20, 1951, ch. 524, § 365 Stat. 573The words “under this section” are substituted for the words “for salvage services rendered”. The words “consider, ascertain, adjust, determine” are omitted as covered by the word “settle”, as defined in . The words “and receive payment of” are inserted for clarity and to conform to . The words “as miscellaneous receipts” are omitted as surplusage.section 4801 of this titlesection 4803 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary of the Army may settle, or compromise, and receive payment of a claim by the United States for salvage services performed by the Department of the Army. Amounts received under this section shall be covered into the Treasury.

children.label: b
children.text: In any case where the amount to be received by the United States is not more than $10,000, the Secretary of the Army may delegate his authority under subsection (a) to any person designated by him.
